_comment: Gauntlet safety wiring (FR-7.3, plan P2). The PreToolUse `command` is an install-tolerant POSIX launcher around the gauntlet-judge-hook console script (installed on PATH by `uv tool install` / `pipx install`, FR-1.1; `gauntlet init` writes this file, FR-1.2). When the hook IS installed the launcher execs it, so the real hook's permission decision and exit code pass through unchanged. The hook is safe-by-default: it gates ONLY gauntlet-run sessions, keyed on GAUNTLET_RUN_ID (engine-injected for the run's lifetime, cleared on stop); outside a run it emits no permission decision and defers to the CLI's normal permission handling — behaving as if absent, never bricked, never force-prompted, regardless of whether a judge is reachable. When the hook is NOT installed (a teammate who never set up Gauntlet) the launcher stays SILENT (exit 0, no per-call 'command not found' notice) unless a gauntlet run is active (GAUNTLET_RUN_ID set), in which case it fails closed (exit 2) so a broken install can never let a run proceed ungated. Shell form runs under POSIX sh — macOS/Linux/WSL2, the project's supported platforms (native-Windows users follow the README's WSL2 path). Under an active gauntlet run the engine sets GAUNTLET_RUN_ID/JUDGE_TOKEN/URL/MODE; unattended runs fail closed on a dead judge, interactive runs fall back to a prompt (review F-004).

hooks.command: command -v gauntlet-judge-hook >/dev/null 2>&1 && exec gauntlet-judge-hook || { if [ -n "${GAUNTLET_RUN_ID:-}" ]; then echo "gauntlet-judge-hook not on PATH during an active gauntlet run; failing closed" >&2; exit 2; fi; exit 0; }
